heading: Houtman Abrolhos vs Hovea — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Houtman Abrolhos, Western Australia and Hovea, Western Australia. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Houtman Abrolhos has a lower overall climate risk score (35/100) compared to Hovea (40/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Houtman Abrolhos or Hovea safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Houtman Abrolhos has a flood risk score of 57/100, while Hovea scores 35/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Houtman Abrolhos scores 22/100 for bushfire risk. Hovea scores 53/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Houtman Abrolhos or Hovea?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Houtman Abrolhos has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.52% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
